name: clone_key_always_zero
code:
proof fn phi_1_clone_key_always_zero(k: SHTKey, out: SHTKey)
    requires
        out == k,
    ensures
        out.ukey == 0,
{
}
reason: If entailed, clone would collapse all keys to zero, destroying key identity

name: ack_clone_empties_unacked
code:
proof fn phi_2_ack_clone_empties_unacked(pre: CAckState, post: CAckState)
    requires
        post@ == pre@,
    ensures
        post.un_acked@.len() == 0,
{
}
reason: If entailed, view-equality would force the un_acked list to always be empty after clone, losing pending messages

name: range_clone_collapses_lo_hi
code:
proof fn phi_3_range_clone_collapses_lo_hi(kr: KeyRange<AbstractKey>, out: KeyRange<AbstractKey>)
    requires
        out == kr,
    ensures
        out.lo == out.hi,
{
}
reason: If entailed, the clone spec would force every key range to be empty, making sharding impossible

name: ack_clone_resets_seqno
code:
proof fn phi_4_ack_clone_resets_seqno(pre: CAckState, post: CAckState)
    requires
        post@ == pre@,
    ensures
        post.num_packets_acked == 0,
{
}
reason: If entailed, view-equality would force the ack counter to zero, losing acknowledgement progress

name: iter_clone_loses_some
code:
proof fn phi_5_iter_clone_loses_some(k: SHTKey, ki: KeyIterator<AbstractKey>, out: KeyIterator<AbstractKey>)
    requires
        ki.k == Some(k),
        out == ki,
    ensures
        out.k is None,
{
}
reason: If entailed, cloning a valid iterator would lose its key, breaking iteration over key ranges
